title: ISic002993. Dedication of church entrance
body:
Text encoding: Jonathan Prag; edition source: https://www.zotero.org/groups/382445/items/4MCP2PVV
1θ(εο)ῦ οἴκο<͡υ>
2εἴσοοδο

Text encoding: Jonathan Prag; edition source: https://www.zotero.org/groups/382445/items/4MCP2PVV
1ΘΥΟΙΚΟ
2ΕΙΣΟΟΔΟ

Facsimile Images

Apparatus criticus

  • Text after Orsi 1903 with suggestions of Ferrua 1989;
  • 1: Ferrua suggests that omicron at the end of line 1 was originally the ligatured form ȣ
  • 2: Ferrua suggests that the lower of the two stacked omicrons in line 2 might be lunate sigma for the end of εἴσοδος (in which case actually line 3)

English translation

Jonathan Prag

Entrance of the house of God

commentary

Orsi declined to resolve the text, which he thought Archaic. Subsequent editions by Pace, Soraci, and Ferrua are all derivative of Orsi's drawing. Pace first proposed the Christian resolution, which would seem to be from above the entrance to a church.

bibliography

  • Orsi NSA 1903 441 dr https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001392
  • Pace 1949 196 dr https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001690
  • Soraci 1958 253 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001571
  • Ferrua 1989 no.470 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001995

Described by Orsi as archaic Greek letters, but seems more likely a Christian text, crudely cut on coarse stone.

Line 1 Interlineation line 1 to 2
